title: Phase 1: Config module docs
description: Update CLAUDE.md to document the new config module and .orchestrator/config.yaml file. Add config.py to the project layout table. Document the config loading behavior and fallback semantics.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md project layout includes config.py with description
CLAUDE.md documents config.yaml location and fallback behavior
Documentation is concise and consistent with existing style

handoff_summary.completed: All acceptance criteria met: config.py in project layout, config.yaml location and fallback behavior documented, documentation is concise and style-consistent.

execution_history.summary: CLAUDE.md already documents the config module fully: config.py appears in the Project Layout table, and the Configuration section covers config.yaml location, load_config/default_config functions, OrchestratorConfig/SchedulerConfig/BackendConfig dataclasses, allowed_tools_for helper, and fallback semantics. All three acceptance criteria are satisfied. No changes required.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
